channel.title: 2026-05-11 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-05-11 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: LLM-Guided Open Hypothesis Learning from Autonomous Scanning Probe Microscopy Experiments
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Boris Slautin, Utkarsh Pratiush, Yu Liu, Kamyar Barakati, Sergei Kalinin
arXiv:2605.06839v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous experimentation has transformed microscopy and materials discovery by enabling closed-loop optimization including imaging and spectroscopy tuning, strucutre property relationship discovery, and exploration of combinatorial libraries. However, most current workflows remain limited to selecting measurements within fixed objective or hypothesis spaces, rather than generating new physical models from experimental data. Here, we introduce an open hypothesis-learning framework that combines symbolic regression with large-language-model-based physical evaluation and implement it for autonomous scanning probe microscopy. Symbolic regression generates candidate analytical relationships directly from sparse measurements, while the language-…

item.title: 超越极性空间群的铁电性再审视
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yudi Yang, Changming Ke, Shi Liu
该综述基于贝里相位现代极化理论，重新审视分数量子铁电性与离子导体铁电性等新兴现象。强调绝缘周期晶体中的极化并非单值矢量，而是模极化量子定义的多值格点量；非极性晶体可具有非零形式极化，连接对称等价结构的绝热路径可在不违反对称原理的情况下产生极化的量子化变化。该多值形式极化的对称性由广义诺伊曼原理支配，长程离子重排可诱导大极化变化，拓展了铁电性的传统对称性范式。

item.title: Pr₂Sn₂O₇中无序诱导的近邻量子自旋冰相
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yi Luo, Brenden R. Ortiz, Miles Knudtson, Stephen D. Wilson, Jue Liu, Benjamin A. Frandsen, Si Athena Chen, Matthias D. Frontzek, Andrey A. Podlesnyak, Joseph A. M. Paddison, Adam A. Aczel
非Kramers稀土离子的磁性烧绿石为探索涌现规范物理和量子自旋冰行为提供了平台，但结构无序对其基态的影响尚不充分理解。本研究结合块体表征和单晶中子散射测量，研究了非Kramers烧绿石Pr₂Sn₂O₇。系统在~1 K以下表现出量子自旋冰物理的关键特征，包括各向异性自旋冰关联和两个不同的动力学时间尺度；进一步冷却至T_f ~ 0.15 K时发生完全自旋冻结转变，伴随完整核Schottky异常的恢复、能隙磁激发的出现以及初现的(100)磁关联的发展。与相关Pr基烧绿石的比较将Pr₂Sn₂O₇置于无序扰动相图的自旋冻结边界附近，建立了无序驱动框架解释量子自旋冰行为如何演化为冻结基态。

item.title: Chordless cycle filtrations for dimensionality detection in complex networks via topological data analysis
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Aina Ferrà Marcús, Robert Jankowski, Meritxell Vila Miñana, Carles Casacuberta, M. Ángeles Serrano
arXiv:2509.08350v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Many complex networks, ranging from social to biological systems, exhibit structural patterns consistent with an underlying hyperbolic geometry. Revealing the dimensionality of this latent space can disentangle the structural complexity of communities, impact efficient network navigation, and fundamentally shape connectivity and system behavior. We introduce a topological data analysis weighting scheme for graphs based on chordless cycles to estimate network dimensionality in a data-driven way. We further show that the resulting descriptors can effectively estimate network dimensionality using a neural network architecture trained on a synthetic graph database constructed for this purpose, which requires no retraining to transfer effecti…

item.title: Gated QKAN-FWP: Scalable Quantum-inspired Sequence Learning
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Kuo-Chung Peng, Samuel Yen-Chi Chen, Jiun-Cheng Jiang, Chen-Yu Liu, En-Jui Kuo, Yun-Yuan Wang, Prayag Tiwari, Andrea Ceschini, Chi-Sheng Chen, Yu-Chao Hsu, Chun-Hua Lin, Tai-Yue Li, Antonello Rosato, Massimo Panella, Simon See, Saif Al-Kuwari, Kuan-Cheng Chen, Nan-Yow Chen, Hsi-Sheng Goan
arXiv:2605.06734v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Fast Weight Programmers (FWPs) encode temporal dependencies through dynamically updated parameters rather than recurrent hidden states. Quantum FWPs (QFWPs) extend this idea with variational quantum circuits (VQCs), but existing implementations rely on multi-qubit architectures that are difficult to scale on noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) devices and expensive to simulate classically. We propose gated QKAN-FWP, a fast-weight framework that integrates FWP with Quantum-inspired Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (QKAN) using single-qubit data re-uploading circuits as learnable nonlinear activation, known as DatA Re-Uploading ActivatioN (DARUAN). We further introduce a scalar-gated fast-weight update rule that stabilizes parameter evolution, su…

item.title: TopoFisher: Learning Topological Summary Statistics by Maximizing Fisher Information
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Matteo Biagetti, Mathieu Carrière, Francesco Conti, Enrico Maria Ferrari, Sven Heydenreich, Karthik Viswanathan
arXiv:2605.07720v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Persistence diagrams provide stable, interpretable summaries of geometric and topological structure and are useful for simulation-based inference when low-order statistics miss key information. Yet persistence-based pipelines require hand-chosen filtrations, vectorizations, and compressors, typically without an objective tied to parameter uncertainty. We introduce TopoFisher, a differentiable persistent-homology pipeline that learns topological summaries by maximizing local Gaussian Fisher information. Using simulations near a fiducial parameter, TopoFisher optimizes trainable filtrations, diagram vectorizations, and compressors without posterior samples or supervised regression targets, while retaining stable topological inductive bias. We…

item.title: Beyond diamond: Interpretable machine learning reveals design principles for quantum defect host materials
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Materials
👤 Mohammed Mahshook and Rudra Banerjee
Author(s): Mohammed Mahshook and Rudra Banerjee Solid-state spin defects in wide-band-gap semiconductors are leading candidates for quantum information processing, but systematic identification of suitable host materials remains limited by the cost of first-principles screening across vast chemical spaces. We address this with a composition-only … [Phys. Rev. Materials 10, 056201] Published Mon May 11, 2026

item.title: Conditional diffusion machine-learning framework for mapping valence electron distribution from convergent beam electron diffraction
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Materials
👤 Lijun Wu, Dmitrii Torbunov, Yihui Ren, and Yimei Zhu
Author(s): Lijun Wu, Dmitrii Torbunov, Yihui Ren, and Yimei Zhu Quantitative convergent beam electron diffraction (QCBED) enables determination of aspherical valence electron (VE) distributions but requires solving a highly nonlinear inverse problem with many parameters, making it time-consuming and difficult to apply to complex systems. Here, the authors introduce a machine-learning framework that replaces traditional refinement. Using a large synthetic CBED dataset generated by Bloch-wave simulations, they train a conditional diffusion model to infer multipole density formalism for VE mapping, from CBED patterns alone. This approach enables practical, high-throughput, and in situ QCBED, opening new opportunities for real-time VE mapping and their correlation with functional responses in…

item.title: Machine learning approach for rapid sample screening in near-surface InAs quantum wells
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Materials
👤 Patrick J. Strohbeen, Abtin Abbaspour, Amara Keita, Tarek Nabih, Aliona Lejuste, Krishna Dindial, Andrea Maiani, Alisa Danilenko, Ido Levy, Jacob Issokson, Tyler Cowan, William M. Strickland, Mehdi Hatefipour, Ashley Argueta, Lukas J. Baker, Melissa Mikalsen, and Javad Shabani
Author(s): Patrick J. Strohbeen, Abtin Abbaspour, Amara Keita, Tarek Nabih, Aliona Lejuste, Krishna Dindial, Andrea Maiani, Alisa Danilenko, Ido Levy, Jacob Issokson, Tyler Cowan, William M. Strickland, Mehdi Hatefipour, Ashley Argueta, Lukas J. Baker, Melissa Mikalsen, and Javad Shabani Semiconductor crosshatch patterns form as a result of strain relaxation and dislocation pileups during growth of lattice mismatched materials. Due to their connection with the internal misfit dislocation network, these patterns are a complex fingerprint of internal strain relaxation and growth aniso… [Phys. Rev. Materials 10, 054601] Published Mon May 11, 2026

item.title: Machine learning study on electronic structure and plasmonic response of graphene/hBN moiré heterostructures
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Leiming Zhang, Yueheng Du, Haotian Sun, and Mingwen Zhao
Author(s): Leiming Zhang, Yueheng Du, Haotian Sun, and Mingwen Zhao We present a unified machine-learning-based framework to investigate the electronic structure and plasmonic response of graphene/hexagonal boron nitride (hBN) moiré heterostructures. This framework integrates machine-learning-derived interatomic potentials and a data-driven Hamiltonian model, facili… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205414] Published Mon May 11, 2026

item.title: 场论原子模拟：学习密度泛函理论的热力学与变分替代模型
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Sambit Das, Bikash Kanungo, Arghadwip Paul, Vikram Gavini
Hohenberg-Kohn定理建立了外势到能量的普适映射，但其在原子模拟中应用受限，且仅适用于电子能量而非热力学良定义的总能量。该工作推导了针对总能量（含电子能与核-核排斥）的类HK定理，以辅助场表述，称为场论原子模拟映射。利用等变架构机器学习该映射，从辅助场的变分精确获得原子力与电子密度，模型在多种基准体系上达到良好精度。

item.title: 从未知量子态最优提取功的学习：指数级降低耗散
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Josep Lumbreras, Ruo Cheng Huang, Yanglin Hu, Mile Gu, Marco Tomamichel
纯量子比特是非平衡自由能源，但状态未知时能量无法完全提取。该研究考虑智能体从无量子存储的N个相同制备纯量子比特序列中提取功，需在最大化当前提取与积累状态知识以改进后续提取之间权衡。利用强化学习的探索-利用权衡，构建完全自适应的功提取协议，使累积耗散随N对数缩放，相比现有策略的平方根缩放实现指数级降低，揭示了强化学习与量子热力学之间的精确联系。

item.title: 基于结构化动作信用与选择性观测的CLI智能体学习
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Haoyang Su, Ying Wen
命令行界面（CLI）智能体作为与演化文件系统、可执行命令行程序和在线执行反馈交互的实用范式正在兴起。近期工作使用强化学习从可验证任务反馈中学习交互能力，但很少利用CLI动作的原生结构化属性作为学习信号。除未充分利用的动作结构外，CLI学习还耦合两个瓶颈：一是从部分观测中识别大型代码库中的任务相关证据，二是将稀疏终端奖励分配给塑造长多轮轨迹的动作。本文通过shell驱动信息提取和文件编辑任务研究这些瓶颈。对于选择性观测，提出σ-Reveal，一种为相同CLI选择token预算上下文的推理时机制。对于信用分配，提出动作优势分配（A³），一种保持标准智能体RL算法复杂度的原生智能体RL方法，从回合级奖励构建轮级优势。

item.title: XDecomposer：多相X射线衍射的无先验集合分解学习
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Hanyu Gao, Bin Cao, Yunyue Su, Tong-Yi Zhang, Qiang Liu
该工作提出XDecomposer，一个无需候选相列表、结构模板或相数先验知识的多相XRD图谱联合分解与识别框架。将多相衍射分析表述为集合预测问题，模型推断无序的相分辨组分集合、混合比例及相应结构表征。相查询驱动的分解机制结合衍射一致的物理重建，实现准确的源分离同时保持晶体学保真度。该框架突破了现有方法假设单相输入而在多相场景中失效的局限。

item.title: Machine-learning-accelerated simulations of vibrational activation for controlled photoisomerization in a molecular motor
item.description:
📚 The Journal of Chemical Physics Current Issue
The precise manipulation of photochemical reactions across broad configurational spaces requires sophisticated design of external control fields. Using the photoisomerization of a molecular motor as a prototype, this study integrates enhanced sampling and active learning to construct accurate machine-learned multi-state potential energy surfaces. By combining active-learning trajectories with enhanced sampling, our approach efficiently covers substantial reaction regions, enabling trajectory propagation extending to tens of picoseconds at a low computational cost within the machine learning framework. Furthermore, local control theory (LCT) is employed to selectively activate specific vibrational motions, leading to accelerated access to reactive regions, enhanced nonadiabatic transitions…

item.title: 基于相变热力学理论精修机器学习势函数
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Paul Fuchs, Julija Zavadlav
基础机器学习势可缓解经典力场在精度与可迁移性上的局限，但参考数据不足和系统偏差导致预测相变温度与实验偏离数百开尔文。该工作提出自上而下的微调策略，引入可微相变温度修正方法，在实验目标压强和温度下最小化两相自由能差。以纯钛在5 GPa以下压强为基准，该方法显著改善了预测相图与液态扩散系数，相变温度与实验值偏差降至数十开尔文。

item.title: Inferring Asteroseismic Parameters from Short Observations Using Deep Learning: Application to TESS and K2 Red Giants
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Nipun Ghanghas, Siddharth Dhanpal, Shravan Hanasoge, Praneeth Netrapalli, Karthikeyan Shanmugam
arXiv:2605.08051v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Asteroseismology is the study of resonant oscillations of stars to infer their internal structure and dynamics. It is also a powerful tool for precisely determining stellar parameters such as mass, radius, surface gravity, and age. The ongoing TESS mission, with its nearly complete sky coverage, presents a unique opportunity to uniformly probe stellar populations across the Milky Way. TESS is estimated to have observed more than 300,000 oscillating red giants, most of which have one to two months of observations. Given the scale of this dataset, we need a fast, efficient, and robust way to analyse the data. In this work, our objective is to develop a machine learning (ML) based method to infer asteroseismic parameters from short-duration o…

item.title: VNN-LIB 2.0: Rigorous Foundations for Neural Network Verification
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ann Roy, Allen Antony, Andrea Gimelli, Matthew L. Daggitt
arXiv:2605.07451v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Neural network verification is an active and rapidly maturing research area, with a growing ecosystem of solvers and tools. The VNN-LIB standard was introduced to support interoperability in this ecosystem, but Version~1.0 has several serious short-comings as a formal foundation: it lacks a precise syntax, semantics, and type system, offers limited expressivity, and relies on externally defined ONNX models whose semantics are informal and constantly evolving. The latter distinguishes VNN-LIB from established standards such as SMT-LIB, where queries are self-contained and have fixed semantics. In this paper we address these challenges by developing the theoretical foundations of VNN-LIB~2.0. Our key contribution is the introduction of the not…

item.title: Learning from Translation: Seasonal Errors and Feature Importance of the ERA5 Turbulence Predictions
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Arial Tolentino, Markus Petters, Luat T. Vuong
arXiv:2605.07981v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Turbulence is a phenomena that is it locally and statistically characterized by measurements, but it is caused by it nonlocal energy cascades associated with the environment. The presence of turbulence coincides with fluctuations in the refractive index, which impact optical sensing, imaging, and signaling applications. Here, we study the machine learning models that predict near-surface optical turbulence strength Cₙ², derived from anemometer-based surface flux measurements through Monin-Obukhov similarity theory, using ERA5 reanalysis data as model inputs. We evaluate the model's ability to perform temporal extrapolation by training on one year of co-located Cₙ² observations and ERA5 data, and applying the model to ERA5 data from other yea…

item.title: Physics Aware Representation Learning on Electronic Charge Density for Materials Property Prediction
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Kammampati Sai Kumar, Albert Linda, Shubham Kumar Maurya, Somnath Bhowmick
arXiv:2605.07227v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The fundamental quantity governing the mechanical and thermodynamic properties of a crystalline solid is its electronic charge density. Yet, its direct use for the rapid prediction of materials properties remains challenging due to its high dimensionality. Here, we present a physics-informed deep learning framework that directly predicts mechanical and thermodynamic properties from the three-dimensional electronic charge density derived from density functional theory (DFT). The proposed approach first utilizes a three-dimensional convolutional autoencoder for unsupervised dimensionality reduction, compressing a high-resolution charge-density grid (128 x 128 x 128) into a compact latent representation (16 x 16 x 16 x 16) while preserving phys…

item.title: Predicting temperature-dependent optoelectronic properties of semiconductor defects with equivariant neural networks
item.description:
📚 npj Computational Materials
👤 Xiangzhou Zhu, Patrick Rinke, David A. Egger
npj Computational Materials, Published online: 11 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41524-026-02111-z Predicting temperature-dependent optoelectronic properties of semiconductor defects with equivariant neural networks

item.title: Stabilized neural Hamilton--Jacobi--Bellman solvers: Error analysis and applications in model-based reinforcement learning
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Minseok Kim, Yeongjong Kim, Namkyeong Cho, Yeoneung Kim
arXiv:2605.07116v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Physics-informed neural solvers offer a promising route to model-based reinforcement learning in continuous time, where optimal feedback synthesis is governed by Hamilton--Jacobi--Bellman (HJB) equations. Practical implementations often occupy a regime that is neither a classical grid method nor a continuous-PDE PINN: the value function is represented by a neural network, finite-difference HJB policy-evaluation operators are evaluated by network queries at shifted points, and residuals are minimized by random continuous collocation. This regime preserves the stabilized finite-difference policy-evaluation structure while avoiding grid-based value unknowns. We develop an error theory for this hybrid regime. Interpreting finite differences as s…

item.title: A fast Physics-Informed Neural Networks based approach to the 2D design of turbine blades
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yuan Huang, Francesca di Mare
arXiv:2605.07131v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Rapid aerodynamic screening of turbomachinery blades across wide operating envelopes remains a major computational bottleneck in preliminary design, particularly for energy-conversion and storage systems such as emerging Carnot batteries. Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) offer a mesh-free alternative to conventional CFD, yet convergence and accuracy often deteriorate for complex blade geometries and off-design flows. We propose a progressive Euler-PINN framework that (i) gradually relaxes boundary conditions from tunnel flow without a blade to full outlet static pressure, and (ii) employs a geometry-aware dynamic loss-weighting scheme that intensifies residual penalties near highly curved boundaries. To the best of our knowledge, thi…

item.title: Geometric Kolmogorov--Arnold Network (GeoKAN)
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Abhijit Sen, Bikram Keshari Parida, Giridas Maiti, Mahima Arya, Denys I. Bondar
arXiv:2605.06740v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce Geometric Kolmogorov--Arnold Networks (GeoKANs), a family of geometry-aware KAN-type models in which approximation is carried out in learned, geometry-adapted coordinates rather than in fixed Euclidean input coordinates. GeoKAN achieves this by learning a diagonal Riemannian metric that warps the input before basis expansion and feature mixing. The learned metric provides a geometric inductive bias through local length scaling and volume distortion, and in physics-informed settings it also affects the differential structure seen by the model. Within this framework, we develop three main variants, namely GeoKAN-NNMetric, GeoKAN-γ, and LM-KAN. For LM-KAN, we further consider three basis-specific versions, LM-KAN-RBF, LM-KAN-Wav, a…

item.title: Learning Minimal-Deviation Corrections for Multi-Dimensional Mismodelling in HEP Simulations
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Matthias Schott, Lucie Flek
arXiv:2605.07460v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate Monte Carlo (MC) modelling in high-energy physics is challenging, particularly in complex scenarios where simulations fail to reproduce observed data. In practice, experimental information is often limited to one-dimensional (1D) distributions, while mismodelling arises in a multidimensional feature space. This restricts traditional correction methods, as one-dimensional reweighting ignores correlations and fully multidimensional approaches require large target datasets. We propose a neural network-based method that operates under these constraints by learning a transformation of simulated events that reproduces the available 1D target distributions while remaining close to the original simulation. This minimal-deviation principle p…

item.title: Physics-based Digital Twins for Integrated Thermal Energy Systems Using Active Learning
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Umme Mahbuba Nabila, Paul Seurin, Linyu Lin, Majdi I. Radaideh
arXiv:2605.06756v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Real-time supervisory control of thermal energy distribution systems requires digital twins that are accurate, interpretable, and uncertainty-aware, yet remain data and computationally efficient. High-fidelity simulations alone are costly, while purely data-driven surrogates often lack robustness. To address these challenges, this work proposes an active learning (AL) framework that couples system-level Modelica simulations with four simpler physics-informed and data-driven surrogate modeling approaches: deterministic Sparse Identification of Nonlinear Dynamics with Control (SINDyC), its probabilistic multivariate-Gaussian extension (MvG-SINDyC), feedforward neural network (FNN), and gated recurrent unit (GRU) network. Tailored to each surro…

item.title: Physics-Informed Reduced-Order Operator Learning for Hyperelasticity in Continuum Micromechanics
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Hamidreza Eivazi, Henning Wessels
arXiv:2605.07738v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Physics-informed operator learning is an attractive candidate for surrogate modeling of microstructures, especially in multiscale finite-element simulations. Its practical use, however, is often limited by the high cost of loss evaluation. We address this bottleneck by combining the Equilibrium Neural Operator (EquiNO) with the QR-based discrete empirical interpolation method (Q-DEIM). EquiNO learns only the modal coefficients of reduced displacement-fluctuation and first Piola-Kirchhoff stress representations built from periodic and divergence-free bases, thereby enforcing periodicity and mechanical equilibrium by construction. Q-DEIM then identifies a small set of spatial points through a column-pivoted QR factorization of the stress basis…

item.title: 物理信息神经网络用于洪水模拟：综述与基准测试案例
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Harry Pearson, Etienne Yntema, Vassilis Glenis, Manuel Herrera
气候变化、土地利用改变和老旧基础设施加剧全球洪水灾害风险，传统数值求解器计算密集且对参数敏感，纯数据驱动模型则外推能力和物理可解释性不足。物理信息神经网络将控制方程嵌入神经网络训练，兼具两者优势。该文系统综述PINNs在洪水模拟中的理论基础、方法进展与新兴应用，并通过三个典型过程导向的基准测试案例强化综合分析。

item.title: 3D tomography of exchange phase in a Si/SiGe quantum dot device
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Dylan Albrecht, Sarah Thompson, N. Tobias Jacobson, Ryan Jock
arXiv:2603.16025v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: The exchange interaction is a foundational building block for the operation of spin-based quantum processors. Extracting the exchange interaction coefficient J(V), as a function of gate electrode voltages, is important for understanding disorder, faithfully simulating device performance, and operating spin qubits with high fidelity. Typical coherent measurements of exchange in spin qubit devices yield a modulated cosine of an accumulated phase, which in turn is the time integral of exchange. As such, extracting J(V) from experimental data is difficult due to the ambiguity of inverting a cosine, the sensitivity to noise when unwrapping phase, as well as the problem of inverting the integral. As a step toward obtaining J(V), we tackle the…

item.title: 基于辅助源时域方法分析GSTC建模超表面的瞬态电磁相互作用
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Minas Kouroublakis, Nikolaos L. Tsitsas, Yehuda Leviatan
本研究提出了辅助源法（MAS）结合广义片层过渡条件（GSTC）的时域公式，用于建模二维超表面的瞬态电磁响应。该方法将频域阻抗型GSTC转换为因果卷积型时域表示，并集成于MAS公式中，为分析超表面与瞬态电磁场的相互作用提供了新的时域计算工具。

item.title: 非共线关联kagome铁磁体MgMn₆Sn₆中的反常磁输运
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Kakan Deb, Sourav Kanthal, Jyotirmoy Sau, Chandra Shekhar, Manoranjan Kumar, Matthias Gutmann, Jhuma Sannigrahi, Nitesh Kumar
该工作报道单晶中子衍射和磁输运性质的联合研究，结合第一性原理计算。中子衍射揭示kagome双层基面内Mn磁矩的非共线排列。霍尔电导显示每层约0.29 e²/h的显著本征贡献，且对磁场取向近乎各向同性；低温下反常霍尔电导发展出明显的各向异性外禀分量。较大的Sommerfeld系数表明存在增强的电子关联。该材料为探索电子拓扑、关联与磁序耦合产生的反常磁输运现象提供了平台。

item.title: 量子临界Dirac半金属中的反常相位相干标度
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sana Nakamichi, Ryotaro Kobara, Yoshinari Unozawa, Yoshitaka Kawasugi, Sakura Hiramoto, Koki Funatsu, Toshio Naito, Masafumi Tamura, Reizo Kato, Yutaka Nishio, Naoya Tajima
作者研究了加压Dirac半金属α-(BEDT-TTF)₂I₃在关联驱动量子相变至电荷有序绝缘态过程中的弱反局域化效应，从低温磁电导评估了相位相干长度Lφ及其温度标度。高压区呈现常规二维退相位行为（Lφ∝T⁻ᵖ，p≈1/2），而趋近临界压强（P_c~1.2 GPa）时指数被抑制至p~0.3，Lφ在0.5 K仍保持700-800 nm。这一反常标度暗示量子临界点附近Dirac电子的非平庸非弹性散射，且弱反局域化跨越相变持续存在支持无能隙或近无能隙量子相变。

item.title: 铁电向列相能量的渐近分析
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Dmitry Golovaty, Peter Sternberg
该工作建立铁电向列相能量泛函与微磁学能量模型之间的数学联系，证明前者通过Γ-收敛退化为具有高弹性各向异性的向列相能量。尽管两类模型形式相似，但其描述的物理机制处于根本不同的参数区间。

item.title: 偏置工程合成反铁磁体在室温零场下稳定亚20 nm斯格明子
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Emily Darwin, Riccardo Tomasello, Reshma Peremadathil Pradeep, Mario Carpentieri, Giovanni Finocchio, Hans J. Hug
该工作提出合成反铁磁（SAF）偏置系统新策略，在零场下稳定铁磁斯格明子（FMsk）与合成反铁磁斯格明子（SAFsk）。通过多层膜设计与预置场循环实现可控的斯格明子形成与极性设定。结合定量高灵敏磁力显微镜与微磁模拟，演示了可靠的零场斯格明子形成，并直接观测到亚20 nm的SAFsk，为迄今报道最小。该补偿型偏置系统抑制畴形成、保持均匀交换场，为斯格明子多层膜提供了可扩展的偏置路径。

item.title: Breaking QAOA's Fixed Target Hamiltonian Barrier: A Fully Connected Quantum Boltzmann Machine via Bilevel Optimization
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jun Liu
arXiv:2605.07473v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: To overcome the limitations of classical partially connected Boltzmann machines and mainstream quantum Boltzmann machines (QBMs), this work extends the conventional circuit of the quantum approximate optimization algorithm (QAOA) to a bilevel optimization architecture and proposes a fully connected QBM. The inner-loop training simulates positive phase energy minimization based on the computational process of the conventional QAOA circuit, whereas the outer-loop training simulates negative phase contrastive divergence learning by optimizing the structural parameters of the target Hamiltonian. It is found that, first, the model exhibits superior performance using only a single layer (p=1) in the QAOA circuit, with an average probability of 0…

item.title: 打破权衡：通过独特链状插层实现高Tc与大层间距的块体二维伊辛超导电性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ziyi Zhu, Leiming Chen, Xiangqi Liu, Haonan Wang, Chen Xu, Ze Yan, Zhengyang Li, Wei Xia, Jiawei Luo, Na Yu, Xia Wang, Ke Qu, Zhenzhong Yang, Yanfeng Guo
该工作通过独特的Ba-S-S-Ba链状结构插层策略，在(BaS)1/3TaS2新多形体中同时实现高超导转变温度与大层间距。该构型打破体c轴镜面对称，实现12.75 Å的超导双层间距（为原始2H-TaS2的三倍以上），有效抑制层间电子耦合，使单层内局域反演对称性破缺主导的伊辛自旋-轨道场得以显现，避免中心对称块体相的典型补偿效应，从而获得稳健的二维伊辛超导电性。

item.title: 手性保护表面自旋波的体介导反射
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Vitaliy I. Vasyuchka, Florin Ciubotaru, Andrii V. Chumak, Burkard Hillebrands, Alexander A. Serga
该工作研究了微米厚度钇铁石榴石薄膜中，Damon-Eshbach型手性表面自旋波在磁介质边界反射时的行为。布里渊光散射谱学、红外热成像和微磁模拟揭示，手性表面波的反射伴随空间局域化的厚度量子化体模式激发，而互易的后向体波则近乎弹性反射。边界处形成驻立体激发，并可量化相关的磁子能量积累与耗散。该结果识别出体模式激发作为手性锁定自旋波反转的物理途径。

item.title: 铁电钙钛矿的竞争相与畴结构：外延(110)生长的优势
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Lan-Tien Hsu, Takeshi Nishimatsu, Anna Grünebohm
应变和畴工程为控制铁电薄膜的相和畴稳定性提供了有力途径。大多数研究聚焦于(100)取向生长，低对称取向的影响尚未充分探索。本文通过第一性原理分子动力学模拟，研究原型铁电钙钛矿在(110)应变下的行为。外延(110)应变确实可能优于广泛研究的(100)取向，因为即使适度的应变值也能稳定一系列具有潜在高功能可调性的亚稳纳米尺度态。该体系中，BaTiO₃和KNbO₃薄膜表现出多畴构型，畴壁法向沿受限面内或弛豫面外方向，并出现异相；PbTiO₃中则观察到复杂的超畴图案和类反铁电畴。这些亚稳纳米尺度构型可能允许大的可逆响应。

item.title: 具有单离子各向异性的XXZ量子自旋链中自旋螺旋的衰减
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Florian Lange, Frank Göhmann, Gerhard Wellein, Holger Fehske
长寿命自旋螺旋态有助于研究量子磁体中的非平衡动力学。本文考虑具有单离子各向异性的反铁磁自旋-S XXZ链中横向自旋螺旋的衰减，利用无限时间演化块截断模拟计算热力学极限下局域磁化的时间演化。尽管单离子各向异性阻止螺旋态成为哈密顿量的本征态，但对于适当选择的波矢仍可长寿命存在；在易轴交换各向异性情况下，单离子各向异性甚至可能稳定螺旋态。自旋波近似给出了最稳定波矢Q的估计条件，与数值结果定性一致。

item.title: 超导相变的有效场理论
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yanyan Bu, Zexin Yang
该工作基于Schwinger-Keldysh形式体系建立了s波超导相变的有效场理论，动力学变量为电磁规范场和复标量序参量。对称性约束的有效作用允许系统处理耗散与涨落，特别探讨了高阶项（含额外动力学场及高阶时间导数）对临界点附近实时动力学的影响。适当截断后可重现Ginzburg-Landau方程，低温相中规范对称性自发破缺导致Higgs模式在临界点附近呈过阻尼扩散行为，而相位涨落通过Higgs机制被规范场吸收。研究还通过全息Schwinger-Keldysh技术进行了严格验证。

item.title: 混合3d/5d重费米子体系CaCu₃Ir₄O₁₂中的涌现动态磁基态
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 J. Ming, Abhisek Bandyopadhyay, G. B. G. Stenning, M. T. F. Telling, N. N. Wang, G. Wang, J. -G. Cheng, D. T. Adroja
该工作结合直流/交流磁化率、比热（低至50 mK）及μ子自旋弛豫（μSR，低至40 mK）研究了立方四重钙钛矿CaCu₃Ir₄O₁₂的真实磁基态。尽管存在强反铁磁相互作用（θW~-200 K），在最低测量温度下未探测到长程磁有序或自旋冻结的 signature。零场与纵向场μSR表征确认了强量子自旋涨落与局域磁矩的真正动态本质，表明该混合3d/5d电子体系具有量子无序磁基态。

item.title: 基于Středa响应的能量分辨量子几何：驱动-耗散玻色子晶格与无序系统
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Anaïs Defossez, Baptiste Bermond, Lucila Peralta Gavensky, Nathan Goldman
该工作证明驱动-耗散玻色子晶格可直接获取积分和能量分辨的Středa响应。方案采用均匀强度、随机相位的受控泵浦结合均匀耗散，产生本征模式占据的洛伦兹滤波器，从而重建粗粒化的能量分辨Středa响应。作为显著应用，该标记阐明了强无序下拓扑能带的命运，捕捉了拓扑安德森绝缘体背后的量子几何结构，为异常谱流和能量分辨量子几何提供了多功能探测平台。

item.title: Entanglement-informed distributed wavefunction approach to scalable quantum many-body systems
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Adriano Amaricci
arXiv:2605.07621v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We show that the entanglement structure of quantum many-body states defines a natural and optimal distributed representation for their simulation. An arbitrary entanglement cut induces a bipartite decomposition of the wavefunction, mapping its distribution onto that of the entanglement spectrum. In this representation the Hamiltonian application, the core of Krylov-subspace methods, reduces to local contractions and communication-optimal operations. Using benchmarks from different methods and models, we demonstrate near-linear scaling for sufficiently large systems and identify entanglement spectrum fragmentation as a key factor controlling computational cost. This establishes entanglement as an organizing principle and unified, method-ind…

item.title: 甲基铵铅碘钙钛矿薄膜中的铁电畴
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Holger Röhm, Tobias Leonhard, Michael J. Hoffmann, Alexander Colsmann
该工作通过压电力显微镜（PFM）研究甲铵铅碘钙钛矿太阳能电池的铁性性质，在垂直与水平PFM成像中发现宽度约90 nm的交替极化畴，确认为极化铁电畴。光照下的高分辨光电导原子力显微图显示交替的载流子抽取图案，归因于铁电畴内局域垂直极化分量。原子力与开尔文探针力显微镜的相关分析证实了这些畴的压电本质。

item.title: d波交错磁体中的有限温度配对密度波超导
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Amrutha N Madhusuthanan, Madhuparna Karmakar
该工作证明交错磁性提供了无需磁场的二维有限动量超导稳定机制。采用非微扰静态路径近似蒙特卡洛方法，显示d波交错磁体支持在强热涨落下仍存在于有限温度窗口的稳健配对密度波（PDW）相。其机制源于动量依赖的自旋劈裂，有效增强了有限质心动量处的配对不稳定性。研究识别了相位相干、能隙闭合与赝能隙形成的不同热力学标度，并确立了PDW态的特征谱学与实空间 signature，为交错磁材料中的热稳定有限动量超导提供了实验可检验的预言。

item.title: 利用动态平均场理论对静态固体中自旋扩散的第一性原理模拟
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Timo Gräßer, Götz S. Uhrig, Matthias Ernst
无序核自旋系综的动力学是核磁共振研究的主题。由于通过空间的长程偶极相互作用，时间演化通常涉及大量自旋，精确暴力计算不可能实现。近期建立的自旋动态平均场理论(spinDMFT)提供了高效且无偏的替代方案，仅需偶极耦合作为输入，适用前提为每个自旋与大量其他自旋相互作用。本文证明spinDMFT可用于描述静态样品中的谱自旋扩散，并模拟迄今缺乏有效定量模拟的零量子线形。对两种测试物质的基准验证与已发表实验数据高度吻合，建议将其用于磁共振各领域重要的大规模自旋扩散模拟。

item.title: 涨落驱动的手性铁磁性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Rokas Veitas, Ahmed Khalifa, Francisco Machado, Shubhayu Chatterjee
作者展示了磁化非守恒耦合如何通过量子涨落稳定手性铁磁体，从根本上改变了铁磁材料中量子涨落被抑制的传统范式。自旋-轨道相互作用修饰经典相图，在经典分析仅预言共线态的区域出现了涨落稳定相，包括具有大轨道手性的铁磁体与手性格子相。这些耦合自发产生标量轨道手性，并表现出增强的热霍尔效应等独特输运特征，与莫尔异质结构、Rydberg原子阵列及非Kramers自旋固体材料直接相关。

item.title: 各向异性kagome反铁磁体Y-kapellasite中的有能隙1/9磁化平台
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Dipranjan Chatterjee, Paul A. Goddard, Ewan R. P. Thomas, Katharina M. Zoch, Hank C. H. Wu, Benjamin M. Huddart, Cornelius Krellner, Edwin Kermarrec, Mladen Horvatić, Steffen Krämer, Pascal Puphal, John Singleton, Stephen J. Blundell, Fabrice Bert
该工作通过单晶脉冲场磁化测量和高场³⁵Cl核磁共振，研究S=1/2各向异性kagome反铁磁体Y₃Cu₉(OH)₁₉Cl₈中的分数磁化平台。识别出场诱导的分数特征层级结构，包括1/3和1/9平台以及较弱的低场特征。¹/₉平台附近的核磁共振谱和磁化率分析表明，该平台伴随有序的局域自旋构型、低能自旋涨落的强烈抑制和激活行为，与有能隙的分数态一致。这些特征与另一观测到该平台的材料YCu₃(OH)₆Br₂[Br₁₋ᵧ(OH)ᵧ]不同，暗示1/9态对底层磁性细节的惊人鲁棒性。

item.title: 巨原子实现的谷极化光子量子光学
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Marcel A. Pinto, Giovanni Luca Sferrazza, Silvia Casulleras, Alejandro Gonzalez-Tudela, Daniele De Bernardis, Francesco Ciccarello
该工作展示利用非局域耦合到光场的二能级发射体（即巨原子），在量子光学中选择性地寻址光子谷。具体考虑与具有子晶格频率失谐的工程蜂窝晶格在多点耦合的量子比特，通过调控耦合点的几何构型，巨原子可选择性发射到单一谷中，发射光子获得明确的谷特征并继承关联的贝里曲率。将量子比特置于相反子晶格失谐区域间的畴壁附近时，发射沿畴壁变为手性的，为电路量子电动力学等平台中实现无需打破电磁介质时间反演对称性的单光子鲁棒手性发射提供了途径。

item.title: Global approximations to correlation functions of strongly interacting quantum field theories
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yuanran Zhu, Yang Yu, Efekan Kökcü, Emanuel Gull, Chao Yang
arXiv:2512.18532v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We introduce a method for constructing global approximations to correlation functions of strongly interacting quantum field theories, starting from perturbative results. The key idea is to employ interpolation method, such as the two-point Padé expansion, to interpolate the weak and strong coupling expansions of correlation function. We benchmark this many-body interpolation approach on two prototypical models: the lattice φ⁴ field theory and the 2D Hubbard model. For the φ⁴ theory, the resulting two point Padé approximants exhibit uniform and global convergence to the exact correlation function. For the Hubbard model, we show that even at second order, the Padé appproximant already provides reasonable characterization of the Matsubara G…

item.title: 阿基米德晶格上量子XY偶极子的基态
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Marcus Bintz, Ahmed Khalifa, Vincent S. Liu, Johannes Hauschild, Michael P. Zaletel, Shubhayu Chatterjee, Norman Y. Yao
该工作通过大规模密度矩阵重正化群（DMRG）计算，研究了描述极性分子与里德伯原子阵列中长程反铁磁相互作用的偶极XY自旋模型在九种阿基米德晶格上的基态性质。四种晶格呈现平庸顺磁态，四种发展出共线Néel磁序；对有序态计算了流体力学参数并与线性自旋波理论比较。三角晶格存在共面磁性、条纹密度波序及可能自旋液体等竞争相；笼目晶格在关联工作中被论证为可能的狄拉克自旋液体。

item.title: 自旋星型网络中噪声测量集中边缘纠缠的对数增长
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jithin G. Krishnan, Harikrishnan K. J., Amit Kumar Pal
在通过XYZ型海森堡相互作用耦合的自旋星型网络中，展示了可局域化双体边缘纠缠(LBPE)随周边尺寸增加和xy各向异性消失而呈对数增长。该特征在xy各向异性非零时消失，表现出各向异性效应，可通过比特局域磁场将系统推离平衡来消除。在模型的大中心和竞争中心极限下，LBPE的行为与大周边极限定性不同。基于部分迹方法计算的双体边缘纠缠在大周边极限下与LBPE定性相似，而在其他两个极限下行为不同。进一步考虑使用非锐利测量对局域化纠缠的广义描述，证明在大周边极限下对数增长存在于所有噪声强度，而在竞争中心极限下不存在。

item.title: Power and limitations of learning quantum dynamics incoherently
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Sofiene Jerbi, Joe Gibbs, Manuel S. Rudolph, Matthias C. Caro, Patrick J. Coles, Hsin-Yuan Huang, and Zoë Holmes
Author(s): Sofiene Jerbi, Joe Gibbs, Manuel S. Rudolph, Matthias C. Caro, Patrick J. Coles, Hsin-Yuan Huang, and Zoë Holmes Quantum process learning is emerging as an important tool to study quantum systems. While studied extensively in coherent frameworks, where the target and model system can share quantum information, less attention has been paid to whether the dynamics of quantum systems can be learned without the sy… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023141] Published Mon May 11, 2026

item.title: 重构氢键网络调控Ru基氢氧化电催化的pH依赖行为
item.description:
📚 Wiley
👤 Lixin Su, Chenxi Cui, Haojie Yu, Yanle Lu, Jinbing Cheng, Liqing Wu, Wenting Li, Wei Luo, Huan Pang
本研究探讨了Ru基催化剂在不同pH条件下氢氧化反应（HOR）活性差异的起源，提出通过重构界面氢键网络解释并调控其pH依赖电催化行为，详情需查阅原文。

item.title: 重构氢键网络调控的Ru基氢氧化电催化的pH依赖行为
item.description:
📚 Wiley
👤 Lixin Su, Chenxi Cui, Haojie Yu, Yanle Lu, Jinbing Cheng, Liqing Wu, Wenting Li, Wei Luo, Huan Pang
该工作通过重构界面氢键网络，阐明Ru基催化剂氢氧化反应(HOR)活性的pH依赖机制，为设计宽pH范围高效HOR电催化剂提供依据。详情请查阅原文。

item.title: A Hardware-aware Hopfield Network with a Nonlinear Memristor Array for Robust Associative Memory with Superlinear Capacity
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Younghyun Lee, Hakseung Rhee, Unhyeon Kang, Seungmin Oh, Kyungmin Lee, Hyun Jae Jang, Seongsik Park, YeonJoo Jeong, Inho Kim, Jong Keuk Park, Kyung Min Kim, Suyoun Lee
arXiv:2605.07223v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Associative memory retrieves complete patterns from partial or corrupted inputs and constitutes a primitive form of generative inference. Classical Hopfield networks (CHN) provide a canonical framework for associative memory but suffer from limited memory capacity. Recently, modern Hopfield networks (MHN) were introduced to achieve higher capacity by using explicit pattern-wise storage and neurons with the softmax activation function, which makes the MHN vulnerable to noise and the hardware implementation complicated due to its network size varying with the number of stored patterns. Here, we introduce a hardware-aware Hopfield network (HHN), in which the intrinsic nonlinear current-voltage characteristics of a charge-trap memristor are leve…

item.title: Physics-informed operator learning for transferable energy-dissipative microstructure dynamics
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jie Xiong, Yue Wu, Xuewei Zhou, Peishuo Zhao, Jiaming Zhu
arXiv:2605.07279v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Phase-field simulations provide mechanistic descriptions of microstructure evolution, but repeated high-fidelity integration over long horizons and broad parameter spaces remains computationally expensive. We present PFNet, a physics-informed neural operator framework that advances microstructural states by learning conditional evolution operators rather than direct correlations. PFNet combines a diffusion-inspired U-Net with periodic padding, entropy-based state conditioning and thermodynamic-parameter modulation to encode boundary consistency, instantaneous ordering state and changes in the free-energy landscape. For Cahn-Hilliard coarsening, PFNet achieves accurate one-step prediction and stable autoregressive rollouts across composition,…

item.title: 碳点–碱式硝酸锌配位网络的高温液相磷光
item.description:
📚 Wiley
👤 Tong Yang, Yi Liu, Yuqi Wu, Xiaodan Yan, Jinlu He, Ting Meng, Zifei Wang
该工作构建了碳点与碱式硝酸锌的配位网络，实现了高温液相条件下的磷光发射，详情需查阅原文。

item.title: 碳点-氢氧硝酸锌配位网络的高温液相磷光
item.description:
📚 Wiley
👤 Tong Yang, Yi Liu, Yuqi Wu, Xiaodan Yan, Jinlu He, Ting Meng, Zifei Wang
该研究构建了碳点与氢氧硝酸锌的配位网络体系，实现了高温液相条件下的磷光发射，拓展了室温及高温液态发光材料的设计策略。具体性能参数见原文。

item.title: Learning density functionals with differentiable DFT
item.description:
📚 Nature Reviews Physics
👤 Antonius von Strachwitz
Nature Reviews Physics, Published online: 11 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s42254-026-00948-3 Antonius von Strachwitz explains the advantages and challenges of replacing the exchange-correlation functionals in DFT with a learnable model.
